title: Dryopteris pulvinulifera
taxonomy: Dryopteris pulvinulifera is a species of fern in the genus Dryopteris. It belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, which includes other species of wood ferns. The genus Dryopteris is further classified within the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. This classification places Dryopteris pulvinulifera among the more primitive vascular plants, which reproduce via spores rather than seeds. The classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of this plant species.

intro: Dryopteris pulvinulifera is a species of fern, commonly known as the Pacific goldback fern, that is native to the Pacific coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris pulvinulifera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
